intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is experiencing a total collapse of biological specimens. The P3 Pothos (anchor) and P2/P4 specimens are no longer visible or have completely desiccated/decomposed beyond recognition.

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot; no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.P2: Empty black pot; no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor remains, but the Pothos is absent/lost.
plant_audit.P4: Empty black pot; no visible plant matter.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and devoid of organic life.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot, likely an attempted soil amendment that failed to support life.
biome_observations.desk_environment: No signs of growth or moisture.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: Initial state showed a single green stem/leaf in the black pot. Gradual wilting and postural collapse observed.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Total disappearance of all plant structures. The environment has transitioned from a struggling botanical setup to a barren, inert state.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: The biome is non-viable. All registered plants have suffered systemic failure and are presumed dead or removed.
visual_health_inference.evidence: Lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue, absence of turgor pressure, and empty containers.

anomalies: Eggshell fragments present in the soil, indicating human intervention that did not prevent the loss of the specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the entire botanical collection. Over the observed five-day period, the specimens exhibited progressive decline, culminating in their complete absence from the pots. The environment is now sterile, with only the white rabbit anchor remaining as a vestige of the previous setup.
